text.title: voice-ui Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the voice-ui methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Sample dialogues (≥3 per intent) MUST be authored BEFORE intent/slot definitions or NLU training. Code-first voice design is forbidden.

rationale.source: Cathy Pearl — Designing Voice User Interfaces

rule.rationale: Dialogues expose missing intents + unnatural prompts; code-first locks in robotic language.

rule.statement: Every intent MUST have a 3-tier error ladder: rephrase prompt → give examples → escalate to fallback (human or alternative modality). One-tier 'I didn't catch that' loops are forbidden.

rationale.source: Cathy Pearl — Designing Voice User Interfaces

rule.rationale: One-tier loops cause abandonment; the 3-tier ladder recovers most failed turns.

rule.statement: Spoken prompts MUST be ≤12 words and reading-grade 8 or below. Long or technical prompts MUST be split.
rule.rationale: Listeners cannot retain >12 words at typical speech rate; grade 8 covers most adult users.

rule.statement: Irreversible actions (delete, send money, purchase) MUST require explicit voice confirmation ('Yes' / 'Cancel'). Silent confirmation is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Silent confirmation creates accidental destructive actions; explicit confirmation is the only safe pattern.

rule.statement: Users MUST be able to interrupt the assistant mid-prompt (barge-in). Long prompts without barge-in are forbidden.

rationale.source: Cathy Pearl — Designing Voice User Interfaces

rule.rationale: Barge-in is the voice analogue of skip / cancel; without it, users feel held hostage.
